title: The Iran War Permanently Altered the Global Economy
summary: The global order has been altered, and economies are unlikely to simply pick up where they left off before the U.S. and Israel began bombing Iran.

title: Gas Prices May Remain High Despite US-Iran Deal
summary: The preliminary agreement may not have an immediate effect on prices at the pump. Damaged infrastructure and risky transport could keep costs up.

title: France, Britain and Other Countries Say They’ll Send Ships to the Strait of Hormuz
summary: For months, a coalition led by Britain and France has been preparing to send minesweepers and other ships to secure the strait once the fighting ends. That moment may finally be here.

title: A Times Investigation Into Epstein’s Death, and Why Gas Prices Might Stay High

summary: At its first game, the team drew spectators who weren’t coming for the soccer, but rather to protest the regime in Tehran.

title: Warsh Faces Tenuous Balancing Act in First Fed Meeting as Chairman
summary: Elevated inflation and the specter of rate increases will greet Kevin M. Warsh in his debut as chairman of the Federal Reserve.

title: How Artisans Are Recreating the American Revolution
summary: In the spirit of 1776, these shipwrights, printers and textile workers are commemorating George Washington’s legacy through 18th-century techniques.

title: A.I. Boom Ignites Asian Chip Companies
summary: They make much of the gear that goes into giant data centers. Demand for their products is shifting the balance of tech power.

title: What to Watch in Primary and Runoff Elections in Georgia, Alabama and Oklahoma
summary: The top race of the day is in Georgia, where Republican voters will pick a nominee to challenge Senator Jon Ossoff, a Democrat.

title: Montana Democrats Are Divided Over How to Win a Republican-held Senate Seat
summary: A rift in Montana between the Democratic nominee and an independent candidate could boost Republican hopes of holding on to an open seat in conservative territory.

title: The Knicks’ Championship Run, in 5 Voices
summary: New Yorkers, from bartenders to teenagers to TV celebrities, turned the city orange and blue in an unusual but welcome sign of unity.

title: Mamdani Is Channeling Knicks’ Fans Euphoria. Will It Work?
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s campaign team used some viral Knicks fan videos as inspiration for his own video strategy to convey a sense of urgency and excitement.

summary: Interceptors show Ukraine’s embrace of autonomous technologies trained on immense troves of wartime data.

summary: With support from Markwayne Mullin and Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the kratom industry is pursuing a potentially lucrative policy. Mr. Mullin owns equity in a company that could benefit.

title: Are You a Morning Person or Night Owl? Take Our Sleep Quiz.
summary: Think you’re a morning person, a night owl or something in between? Take our “chronotype” quiz to find out.

summary: In a country where the housing market is squeezed, decommissioned electrical substations are in demand as refurbished residences.
